name: Knee MRI
description: MRI of the knee for evaluation of menisci, ligaments, and joint pathology

findings.technique: MRI of the knee was performed in multiple planes including T1, T2, PD, and STIR sequences.
findings.comparison: No prior studies available for comparison.

sections.name: Anterior Cruciate Ligament
sections.content: The ACL demonstrates [intact / no tear / no fiber discontinuity]. The ACL is [midline / no angulation].

sections.name: Posterior Cruciate Ligament
sections.content: The PCL demonstrates [intact / no tear / no fiber discontinuity]. The PCL is [taut / normal].

sections.name: Medial Meniscus
sections.content: The medial meniscus demonstrates [no tear / no degeneration / no horizontal tear / no bucket-handle tear]. The meniscal body and horns are [intact / normal].

sections.name: Lateral Meniscus
sections.content: The lateral meniscus demonstrates [no tear / no degeneration / no horizontal tear / no bucket-handle tear]. The meniscal body and horns are [intact / normal].

sections.name: Medial Collateral Ligament
sections.content: The MCL demonstrates [intact / no tear / no thickening / no sprain]. The proximal and distal insertions are intact.

sections.name: Lateral Collateral Ligament
sections.content: The LCL demonstrates [intact / no tear / no thickening / no sprain]. The fibular collateral ligament is intact.

sections.name: Patellar Tendon
sections.content: The patellar tendon demonstrates [no tear / no thickening / no tendinopathy]. The tibial tubercle is [intact].

sections.name: Quadriceps Tendon
sections.content: The quadriceps tendon demonstrates [no tear / no thickening / no tendinopathy]. The patellar attachment is intact.

sections.name: Bone
sections.content: The femoral condyles, tibial plateau, and patella demonstrate [no fracture / no bone bruise / no osteochondral lesion]. No joint effusion.

sections.name: Articular Cartilage
sections.content: The articular cartilage over the femoral condyles, tibial plateau, and patella demonstrates [normal thickness / no defect / no degeneration].

impression:
1. Normal knee MRI.
2. No meniscal tear, ligamentous injury, or osteochondral lesion identified.
author: RadReport Templates
